rag_rules: Fix unresolved references by ensuring all identifiers are visible in the current scope or declared as helpers within the same annex.
Resolve type mismatches by aligning helper declarations with the correct AGREE scalar types (int, real, bool).
Avoid placing guarantee statements in implementation scopes; use only valid output-to-input dependencies in component type contracts.
Do not introduce package-qualified identifiers that are not present in the analyzed model's dependencies.
generic_rules: When generating AGREE contracts, ensure all referenced identifiers are either visible features or properly declared helpers.
Use only AGREE scalar types (int, real, bool) for constant and equation declarations to prevent type resolution errors.
Place implementation-level bindings in implementation scopes and component type contracts in component type scopes.
Validate that all expressions in guarantees have consistent types to avoid '<error>' type mismatches.
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: AGREE contracts must be enclosed in `annex agree {** ... **};` blocks.
Assignments in implementation scopes must reference existing output features or declared auxiliary variables.
Guarantee expressions should relate visible inputs to visible outputs, not subcomponent paths.
Type declarations in AGREE must match AADL data types: int, real, bool.
anti_patterns: Do not reference subcomponent ports in component type annexes unless represented through valid variables or outputs.
Do not place guarantee statements in implementation scope; use type scope for output dependency contracts.
Do not introduce package-qualified identifiers that are not declared in the model or its configured dependencies.
Do not attempt to fix type errors by adding new architecture; instead, resolve root causes or remove invalid clauses.
